framing: Veterinary surgeon transitioning to animal health biotech product development role in Melbourne

jd.title: Product Development Manager — Companion Animal Diagnostics
jd.style_note: Graduate-friendly/corporate: explains business context, values clinical expertise, flexible on exact product experience
jd.about: IDEXX is a global leader in veterinary diagnostics, software, and water testing. Our Melbourne team develops next-generation point-of-care diagnostic tests for companion animals. We're looking for someone who combines clinical veterinary knowledge with product development instincts.

requirements.text: DVM/BVSc or equivalent veterinary degree with clinical practice experience
5+ years of combined experience in veterinary clinical practice and/or veterinary diagnostics industry
Strong understanding of companion animal clinical pathology (haematology, biochemistry, urinalysis, cytology)
Experience interpreting diagnostic test results and understanding sensitivity/specificity trade-offs
Experience with product development lifecycle: from concept through validation to market launch
Knowledge of in-vitro diagnostic (IVD) regulatory requirements (TGA, FDA 510(k), or EU IVDR)
Experience with lateral flow immunoassay or ELISA development
Published research in veterinary clinical pathology or diagnostics

responsibilities: Lead product development for new companion animal point-of-care diagnostic tests (SNAP platform)
Define clinical requirements and performance specifications based on veterinary practice workflows
Design and execute clinical validation studies with partner veterinary hospitals across Australia and New Zealand
Collaborate with R&D engineers on assay development and prototype testing
Support TGA regulatory submissions and CE marking documentation for new diagnostic products

expected_output.summary: Veterinary surgeon with 8 years of small animal clinical practice experience in Melbourne, most recently Senior Veterinarian at a multi-vet referral hospital. Specialised in internal medicine and diagnostic imaging. Extensive daily experience with IDEXX in-house analysers (Catalyst One, ProCyte Dx, SediVue) and SNAP point-of-care tests. Published 2 case reports in Australian Veterinary Journal. Member of Australian Veterinary Association (AVA). Seeking career transition from clinical practice to veterinary diagnostics product development.

experience.title: Senior Veterinarian — Internal Medicine
bullets: Managed caseload of 20+ patients/day with focus on internal medicine, endocrinology, and gastroenterology
Interpreted 50+ diagnostic panels daily using IDEXX Catalyst One (biochemistry), ProCyte Dx (haematology), and SediVue (urinalysis)
Performed and interpreted cytology, fine needle aspirates, and bone marrow aspirates for oncology referrals
Mentored 4 new graduate veterinarians on diagnostic workup protocols and clinical pathology interpretation
Published 2 case reports in Australian Veterinary Journal on atypical endocrine presentations

experience.title: Veterinary Surgeon (promoted from New Graduate, 2019)
bullets: Provided primary care veterinary services including surgery, dentistry, and preventive medicine
Used IDEXX SNAP tests (4Dx Plus, cPL, fPL, Parvo) daily as first-line point-of-care diagnostics
Managed client communication around diagnostic results and treatment planning
Completed Membership examination (MANZCVS) in Small Animal Internal Medicine (2020)

certifications: DVM — Doctor of Veterinary Medicine, University of Melbourne (2013-2017)
MANZCVS (Small Animal Internal Medicine) — Australian and New Zealand College of Veterinary Scientists (2020)
Veterinary Surgeons Board of Victoria — Registration (current)
education: DVM — Doctor of Veterinary Medicine, University of Melbourne (2013-2017)

jd_requirements: DVM BVSc veterinary degree clinical practice
5+ years veterinary clinical practice diagnostics industry
companion animal clinical pathology haematology biochemistry urinalysis cytology
product development lifecycle concept validation market launch
IVD regulatory TGA FDA 510(k) EU IVDR
published research veterinary clinical pathology diagnostics
deliberate_flaws: No product development experience — entire career in clinical veterinary practice
No IVD regulatory experience (TGA, FDA 510(k), EU IVDR)
No lateral flow immunoassay or ELISA development experience (uses SNAP tests, doesn't develop them)
No industry experience — veterinary practice is different from diagnostics company R&D
No product lifecycle management experience (concept, validation, launch)
No clinical validation study design experience (published case reports, not validation studies)
No engineering or R&D collaboration experience
No experience with assay performance metrics beyond clinical interpretation (sensitivity/specificity from user perspective, not development perspective)
Career changer — clinical practice to product development requires significant mindset shift
Research output is case reports (2), not peer-reviewed diagnostic validation studies
No experience with regulatory submissions or technical documentation
